steps.title: Identify the subject's number
steps.content: The subject is the average processing power (singular). The parenthetical phrase "(now often containing dozens of cores)" elaborates on microchips, not the subject — strip it out and the structure is clear: "the average processing power ______ dramatically."

steps.title: Apply subject-verb agreement and tense
steps.content:
The subject is singular, and "since then" (spanning from the early 2000s to the present) requires present-perfect tense.

A) has improved — singular present-perfect; matches the singular subject and the ongoing-to-now timeframe. Correct.
B) have improved — plural present-perfect; disagrees with singular subject. Wrong.
C) have been improving — plural present-perfect progressive; disagrees with singular subject. Wrong.
D) are improving — plural simple present; disagrees with singular subject and doesn't capture the since-then span. Wrong.

A is correct.
